config.exam_name: 48 Databricks GIT Folders

question.line: What is the primary purpose of Databricks Git Folders?

options.A: To automatically create Delta Live Tables pipelines
options.B: To integrate Git repositories with a Databricks workspace for version control and collaboration
options.C: To replace Unity Catalog for data governance

question.line: Which Git operation is supported by Databricks Git Folders?
Which Azure DevOps service is used to store notebooks, source code, and configuration files using Git version control?
How does Databricks authenticate with Azure DevOps when connecting a Git repository?
What is the purpose of the Sparse Checkout feature?

options.B: To clone only selected folders from a repository, reducing the local workspace size
options.C: To convert notebooks into Delta tables
options.D: To back up notebooks to Unity Catalog Volumes
options.E: To encrypt Git repositories before cloning

question.line: Why are feature branches commonly used when working with Databricks Git Folders?

options.A: They permanently replace the main branch.
options.B: They allow developers to work independently without affecting the main branch.
options.C: They automatically deploy code to production.
options.D: They eliminate the need for commits.
options.E: They prevent pulling updates from the remote repository.

question.line: Why does the lesson recommend saving Databricks notebooks in Source format instead of the default .ipynb format?

options.A: Source format automatically executes notebooks faster.
options.B: Source format stores notebooks as .py files, making Git diffs, code reviews, and version control easier.
options.C: Source format converts notebooks into SQL scripts only.
options.D: Source format eliminates the need for Git repositories.
options.E: Source format enables automatic deployment to production.

question.line: After modifying a notebook, what indication does Databricks display before the changes are committed?
Which sequence correctly describes the Git workflow demonstrated in the lesson?

options.B: Create Notebook → Edit Notebook → Commit → Push
options.D: Create Branch → Delete Repository → Commit → Pull
options.E: Create SQL Warehouse → Commit → Push → Publish Dashboard

question.line: Which tools are highlighted as part of the future CI/CD deployment workflow for Databricks projects?

options.A: Azure Pipelines, Databricks Asset Bundles (DABs), and Databricks CLI
options.B: Unity Catalog, Delta Sharing, and MLflow
options.C: SQL Alerts, Genie Spaces, and Metric Views
options.D: Apache Hive, Apache NiFi, and Kafka
